currentUse: Active Protestant house of worship — Grace Gospel Church, a non-denominational congregation founded in Paterson in 1982, holds Sunday school (9am), Sunday worship (10am), and weekday prayer/Bible study at the former St. Casimir property on Montgomery Street.
summary: The 1962 St. Casimir church building at 147 Montgomery Street, Paterson, still stands and is in active use by Grace Gospel Church, a non-denominational Protestant congregation founded locally in 1982, whose Facebook page identifies its location as 147 Montgomery Street and whose live website (which lists 139 Montgomery Street, an adjacent address consistent with the former parish complex) publishes a current weekly worship schedule. The Global True Lithuania heritage survey confirms the parish closed in 2014 and was sold to non-denominational Christians. Lithuania's State Cultural Heritage Commission (VKPK) documents the site on its heritage map of Lithuanian churches abroad, noting the 1912 wooden church no longer survives, that the 1962 building (S.A. Seiler firm, with input from Lithuanian architects) carried its Lithuanian character chiefly in a traditional Lithuanian cross, and that the building "no longer belongs to the Lithuanian community." Stale Catholic directory listings (GCatholic, Discover Mass) still show St. Casimir at the address, but no active Catholic parish operates there. No sale price, deed record, or press coverage of the 2014 closing could be located online, and no evidence of reunions or organized preservation efforts was found beyond the VKPK heritage documentation.

developments.headline: Parish closed; property passed to Grace Gospel Church
developments.detail: The Global True Lithuania survey of New Jersey Lithuanian heritage records the church at 147 Montgomery Street as 'closed 2014, sold to non-denominational Christians.' Grace Gospel Church, a non-denominational Paterson congregation founded in 1982 by Rev. William H. Butler, is the occupant — its Facebook page carries the name 'Grace Gospel Church, 147 Montgomery Street, Paterson NJ 07501.'

sources.title: New Jersey: Lithuanians and Lithuanian heritage
Grace Gospel Church, 147 Montgomery Street, Paterson NJ 07501 (Facebook page)

developments.headline: Building in active use by Grace Gospel Church
developments.detail: Grace Gospel Church's live website publishes a current schedule — Sunday school 9am, Sunday worship 10am, Monday evening prayer 6-7pm, Tuesday Bible study 7-8pm — listing its address as 139 Montgomery Street (adjacent to the 147 church address, consistent with the former parish complex of church and rectory). The building is standing and occupied, not vacant or demolished.

developments.headline: Documented on Lithuania's state heritage map of churches abroad
developments.detail: The Valstybinė kultūros paveldo komisija (Lithuanian State Cultural Heritage Commission) maintains an entry for Šv. Kazimiero bažnyčia Patersone on its map of Lithuanian heritage sites abroad: Lithuanian settlement from 1892, a modest wooden church built 1912 (no longer surviving), the current church opened 1962 (S.A. Seiler firm; design variants by Jonas Mulokas considered), with Lithuanian character expressed chiefly through a traditional Lithuanian cross. It notes the building still stands but no longer belongs to the Lithuanian community. This is the only preservation-adjacent activity found.

sources.title: New Jersey: Lithuanians and Lithuanian heritage
Grace Gospel Church, 147 Montgomery Street, Paterson NJ 07501 (Facebook page)
Church of St. Casimir, Paterson, New Jersey, USA

conflictsWithArchiveRecord: No contradictions. The archive record is corroborated: closed 2014, sold to Grace Gospel Church (Protestant/non-denominational), current building 1962. Two refinements: (1) GCatholic dates the parish's history to 1911 and VKPK dates Lithuanian settlement to 1892 with the wooden church built 1912 — consistent with the archive's 'founded 1912 / first Lithuanian church 1912' but suggesting parish organization may have begun in 1911; (2) VKPK adds that the 1912 wooden church no longer survives and attributes the 1962 building to the S.A. Seiler firm with Lithuanian architect involvement (Mulokas variants considered), with a Lithuanian cross as the main ethnic design element. The archive's 'church and rectory sold' pairing fits the two adjacent addresses now used by Grace Gospel (147 and 139 Montgomery St), though which parcel was the rectory is unverified.
gaps: Sale price, deed date, and transaction details were not found: web searches and an NJParcels.com query returned no parcel data for 147 Montgomery Street (the site notes owner information is no longer provided by its government sources); Passaic County land records would require a direct registry search. No contemporaneous press coverage of the March 2, 2014 final Mass or a diocesan closing announcement was located online (the Diocese of Paterson parish list at rcdop.org returned HTTP 403 and could not be checked for St. Casimir's removal). The relationship between Grace Gospel's two addresses (139 on its website vs. 147 on its Facebook page) — presumably former rectory and church — is unverified. No reunions, alumni activity, or organized preservation campaigns for the Paterson Lithuanian community were found for 2015-2026. The condition of the interior and whether the Lithuanian cross or other ethnic elements survive under Grace Gospel's occupancy is unknown. Identity confirmation: the Lithuanian match is secure — VKPK's Lithuanian-heritage entry and Global True Lithuania both describe this exact church at 147 Montgomery St.
